major: Environmental Economics and Policy, Lower Division B.S.
notes: ABOUT THE ENVIRONMENTAL ECONOMICS & POLICY MAJOR This major offers an opportunity to explore aspects of economic and political institutions that affect the development and management of natural resources and the environment. The program takes a problem-solving approach to issues involving renewable and fixed natural resources, and it is based on a foundation in micro-economic theory and the economics of resources and the environment. For additional information, please contact the Environmental Economics & Policy advisor at eep.ugrad@berkeley.edu .

The Rausser College of Natural Resources (RCNR) admits students directly into one of its nine undergraduate majors as selected by the applicant at the time of application. Because Rausser College does not admit transfer students as undeclared, each individual major has a distinct set of transfer courses required as major preparation before the applicant is minimally eligible for admission. Resources for RCNR transfer applicants: UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admissions Minimum Requirements for Transfer Admission Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page - includes transfer admission guidelines specific to each Rausser College major, as well as additional information on how to submit a competitive application for your intended major Rausser College Major Requirements and Sample Degree Plans Rausser College Advising Staff

Please Note: Applicants are expected to complete all required coursework by the end of the spring term prior to admission. Majors may allow one course pending in the summer term prior to admission if the major has more than five required courses and at least five courses of the required coursework have been completed; in this case, if a requirement includes both a lecture and lab, the lecture and lab together are treated as one course. Majors which require five or fewer required courses to transfer will not allow required coursework to be completed in the summer prior to admission. Please see the Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page for individual requirements by major. Environmental Economics & Policy applicants have five or fewer required courses to complete prior to transfer and will not be allowed to complete a required course in the summer prior to admission. To ensure full articulation of transfer coursework: if a series of courses at a community college is required (e.g., Chemistry 101 + 102 + 103 = CHEM 1A/1AL + CHEM 1B at UC Berkeley), all of the courses in the series must be completed, and must (unless otherwise indicated) be completed at the same community college. Partial completion of the series (e.g., two of the three required courses) may result in no credit toward the requirement(s), and splitting a series of courses between different community colleges is not guaranteed to satisfy the requirement. Courses taken out of series will be evaluated by college faculty during the transfer admissions process. If you have questions about the articulation of a series for your intended major, please email eep.ugrad@berkeley.edu . Minimum Admissions Requirements in Environmental Economics & Policy: Equivalent of: ENGLISH R1A and ENGLISH R1B - satisfied by full UC IGETC, or may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit ENVECON 1, ECON 1, ECON 2, or ECON 3 - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit MATH 16A and MATH 16B; or MATH 1A and MATH 1B - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit Highly Recommended (but not required) Prior to Transfer 7-course breadth coursework or full UC IGETC
